JD_HW6

Jennifer Deaton

2026-04-02

load libraries

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

load file

load("/Users/jenniferdeaton/Desktop/DSC 502/HW 6/house_prices.rda")

get year from date

house_prices = house_prices %>% mutate(year = as.numeric(format(date, "%Y")))

create plot of house prices per state and mark years using facet_wrap

houseprices = ggplot(house_prices, aes(x = year, y = house_price_index, group = state)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ state) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020), labels = c("'80", "'00", "'20"), limits = c(1980, 2020)) + labs(title = "House Price Index Trends by State", x = "Year", y = "House Price Index") + theme_minimal()

show plot

houseprices
## Warning: Removed 2448 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

use gather to create measure and value for house_price_index and unemployment_perc, exclude other values

house_reshaped = gather(house_prices, key = measure, value = value, -date, -state, -house_price_perc, -year)

view head to confirm measure and value exist

head(house_reshaped)
## # A tibble: 6 × 6
##   date       state   house_price_perc  year measure       value
##   <date>     <chr>              <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>
## 1 1976-01-01 Alabama           0.0404  1976 unemploy_perc   6.7
## 2 1976-02-01 Alabama           0.0518  1976 unemploy_perc   6.7
## 3 1976-03-01 Alabama           0.0593  1976 unemploy_perc   6.6
## 4 1976-04-01 Alabama           0.0596  1976 unemploy_perc   6.5
## 5 1976-05-01 Alabama           0.0557  1976 unemploy_perc   6.4
## 6 1976-06-01 Alabama           0.0536  1976 unemploy_perc   6.5

use plot from part a to view house_reshaped for house_price_index and unemploy_perc

housepricesreshaped = ggplot(house_reshaped, aes(x = year, y =  value, color = measure, group = measure)) +  geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ state) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020), labels = c("'80", "'00", "'20"), limits = c(1980, 2020)) + labs(title = "House Price Index Trends by State", x = "Year", y = "House Price Index") + theme_minimal()

view plot

housepricesreshaped
## Warning: Removed 4896 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

# the above plots are not great plots in my opinion. rating percentage on the same scale as how much houses cost is a bad comparisonas the graphs do not accurately show the percentage as the other values are going towards 300 while percentage is to 1 so you can’t really effectively see it. it’s also confusing to have 51 different graphs. This could possibly have been better by comparing the percent increase or decrease of housing costs to the percentage of unemployment increase or decrease per state or even on a national average.
